page: Microsoft Azure E64as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine configured with 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz, the instance features 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is dedicated, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score and strong ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark database operations. Redis SET operations reached 63.15 million operations per second. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks remains average, with read speeds at 119.34 GB/sec and write speeds at 61.21 GB/sec. Large-scale LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck, with the 70B model text generation scoring in the bottom 10% at 2.47 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64as_v5 offers a high-density memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the added expense of local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware. The server is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM text generation or workloads requiring high-speed local scratch disks.
description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, running on a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 32 physical cores, this Hyper-V virtualized instance provides an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier memory scores and strong multi-core capabilities, including 63.15 million Redis SET operations per second and high multi-threaded decompression speeds. However, large-block uncached memory bandwidth is average, and large LLM text generation performance is poor. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and in-memory caching, offering a balanced, high-density memory profile without the overhead of local disk or GPU hardware.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v5 is an EASv5 virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It is optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v5 is an EASv5 virtual machine with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a high memory-to-core ratio for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
Equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio
Top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core processing capabilities
No local storage or GPU included, but supports Premium Storage
Optimized for database operations, in-memory caching, and software compilation
